# llms.txt - talktomeclaude
# Machine-readable instructions for AI assistants installing this tool for a user.
# Format: YAML blocks for structured data, markdown for context.

## METADATA
```yaml
name: talktomeclaude
description: Local-first voice medium for Claude Code. You speak (local Whisper-class STT); Claude answers back in a real voice speaking ONLY its dialogue (never tool calls, code, or logs) via a Claude Code plugin Stop hook.
repo: https://github.com/Fredasterehub/talktomeclaude
license: MIT
version: 0.0.1
entrypoint: talktomeclaude   # console script installed into the venv
```

## REQUIREMENTS
```yaml
python: ">=3.11"
clone_size: "~5 MB"            # voice models are NOT committed to git; fetched on first use (cache-on-demand)
first_run_downloads:           # each downloaded ONCE then reused; all under ~/.cache/talktomeclaude/
  tts_voices: "~250 MB total — 3 public-domain Piper voices from HF rhasspy/piper-voices, on first `speak`. Pre-fetch: talktomeclaude voices --download"
  stt_model:
    cpu_tier: "~486 MB (small.en)"
    gpu_tier: "~3 GB (large-v3, needs NVIDIA + CUDA)"
  cache_dir: "~/.cache/talktomeclaude/"
system_packages:
  linux: [libportaudio2, git]   # dnf: portaudio · pacman: portaudio  (PortAudio powers mic/speaker via sounddevice)
  macos: []                     # git via Xcode Command Line Tools (auto-prompt on first git use)
  windows: []                   # see GOTCHAS for the Stop-hook / Git Bash note
depends_on_optional:
  claude_code: "required only for the voice loop + Stop-hook plugin; the CLI works standalone"
```

## INSTALL_WINDOWS
```yaml
# Windows Terminal (PowerShell)
steps:
  - cmd: 'powershell -ExecutionPolicy ByPass -c "irm https://astral.sh/uv/install.ps1 | iex"'   # install uv (also fetches Python)
  - note: "get the code — Code > Download ZIP and extract, OR: winget install --id Git.Git -e; git clone https://github.com/Fredasterehub/talktomeclaude.git"
  - cmd: 'cd talktomeclaude'
  - cmd: 'uv venv --python 3.12'
  - cmd: 'uv pip install -e .'
  - cmd: '.\.venv\Scripts\talktomeclaude speak "Hello from Claude."'   # verify
```

## INSTALL_MACOS
```yaml
# Terminal.app (Apple Silicon or Intel). No CUDA on macOS -> STT runs the CPU tier (expected).
steps:
  - cmd: 'curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh'   # install uv (also fetches Python)
  - cmd: 'git clone https://github.com/Fredasterehub/talktomeclaude.git'   # first git run prompts to install Command Line Tools
  - cmd: 'cd talktomeclaude'
  - cmd: 'uv venv --python 3.12 && uv pip install -e .'
  - cmd: './.venv/bin/talktomeclaude speak "Hello from Claude."'   # verify
```

## INSTALL_LINUX
```yaml
steps:
  - cmd: 'sudo apt install -y libportaudio2 git'   # dnf: portaudio git · pacman: portaudio git
  - cmd: 'curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh'   # install uv (also fetches Python)
  - cmd: 'git clone https://github.com/Fredasterehub/talktomeclaude.git'
  - cmd: 'cd talktomeclaude'
  - cmd: 'uv venv --python 3.12 && uv pip install -e .'
  - cmd: 'uv pip install -e ".[cuda]"'   # OPTIONAL: NVIDIA GPU tier for STT (large-v3)
  - cmd: './.venv/bin/talktomeclaude speak "Hello from Claude."'   # verify
```

## PLUGIN_CLAUDE_CODE
```yaml
# Registers a Stop hook that speaks Claude's reply after each turn.
test:      'claude --plugin-dir .'      # from the project directory, loads the plugin for the session
permanent: '/plugin install .'          # run INSIDE Claude Code to keep it
mute:      'talktomeclaude assist off'  # full mute (hook stays silent); 'assist on' to re-enable
```

## TOPOLOGY
```yaml
# Modular — accommodates any install. Pick per machine:
all_local:
  desc: "mic + Claude Code + speakers all on ONE machine (e.g. a Mac/PC laptop)."
  setup: "default; no extra config. Just `talktomeclaude listen`."
remote_ssh:
  desc: "voice on the machine you sit at; Claude Code runs on a server (e.g. SSH into Proxmox/VPS). Server needs NO audio; client needs NO Claude Code."
  how: "mic capture + STT + spoken reply stay local; only text crosses SSH. POSIX clients use a multiplexed connection; native Windows omits unsupported Unix control-socket options. claude runs on the server via a login shell."
  setup:
    - "server: install + log into Claude Code (claude --version works)."
    - "client: install talktomeclaude; set up passwordless SSH keys to the server (required, or it prompts every utterance)."
    - "client: talktomeclaude config set remote user@host   # persist once"
    - "client: talktomeclaude config set remote-cwd /srv/project   # optional; remote project directory"
    - "client: talktomeclaude listen   # per-run overrides: listen --remote user@host --remote-cwd /srv/project"
  revert: "talktomeclaude config set remote local"
  clear_project_dir: "talktomeclaude config set remote-cwd home"
```

## USAGE
```yaml
commands:
  companion: 'talktomeclaude companion'              # Windows Stage A desktop companion (Tk + Win32)
  companion_headless: 'talktomeclaude companion --headless'  # production-backed recovery surface
  legacy_tui: 'talktomeclaude tui'                   # explicit compatibility dashboard
  speak:      'talktomeclaude speak "text" [--out file.wav] [--voice NAME]'
  listen:     'talktomeclaude listen [--mode always-on|push-to-talk|push-toggle] [--remote user@host] [--remote-cwd PATH] [--once]'
  transcribe: 'talktomeclaude transcribe AUDIO [--device auto|cuda|cpu] [--show-tier]'
  filter:     'talktomeclaude filter transcript.jsonl'   # prints only spoken dialogue (never code/tools)
  voices:     'talktomeclaude voices [--download]'       # list voices + licenses; --download pre-fetches all
  config_set: 'talktomeclaude config set recording-mode|voice-assist|remote|remote-cwd VALUE'
  config_get: 'talktomeclaude config get recording-mode|voice-assist|remote|remote-cwd'
  assist:     'talktomeclaude assist on|off|status'
default_recording_mode: push-to-talk
windows_companion_recording_mode: push-toggle
windows_companion_hotkey: Ctrl+Alt+Space
stage_a_default: 'No-argument talktomeclaude still opens the legacy dashboard until the Windows physical gate passes.'
```

## WINDOWS_COMPANION
```yaml
support: Windows 11 only
delivery_contract: "The operator focuses an eligible terminal and places its blinking cursor. Finish-toggle snapshots that foreground target for one delivery, revalidates it before clipboard/paste/optional Enter, then discards it. It never pins or retargets a terminal across turns."
auto_submit_warning: "Auto-submit sends Enter to the eligible foreground terminal captured at finish-toggle; the operator is responsible for the intended tab, pane, shell, and cursor position."
recovery:
  - talktomeclaude companion --headless
  - talktomeclaude tui
repository_guide: https://github.com/Fredasterehub/talktomeclaude/blob/main/docs/WINDOWS_COMPANION.md
remote_helper: "Remote companion mode requires TalkToMeClaude on the Claude host and its console script on PATH for `hook install` and `hook stream`; the older listen topology does not. The remote still needs no audio stack."
```

## VOICES
```yaml
# All public domain (trained from scratch; no copyrighted/celebrity voices). Fetched on first use from
# HF rhasspy/piper-voices, cached at ~/.cache/talktomeclaude/voices (pre-fetch: talktomeclaude voices --download).
# Piper runs as a subprocess (keeps project MIT).
available:
  - {name: en_US-ljspeech-high, accent: en_US, quality: high}
  - {name: en_GB-cori-medium,   accent: en_GB, quality: medium}
  - {name: en_US-bryce-medium,  accent: en_US, quality: medium}
```

## GOTCHAS
```yaml
windows_stop_hook:
  problem: "The Stop hook is a bash .sh script; native Windows may not execute it (can open it in an editor)."
  fix: "Install Git for Windows (winget install --id Git.Git -e) so Claude Code routes hooks through Git Bash. The CLI (speak/listen/transcribe/voices) works regardless; only the auto-speak hook needs Git Bash."
windows_terminal_and_ssh:
  note: "Native Windows listen uses stdlib msvcrt for push-to-talk/push-toggle; always-on needs no console key API. Windows OpenSSH runs without Unix ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist options; POSIX keeps multiplexing."
macos_microphone:
  problem: "First use of listen/recording: macOS asks Terminal for Microphone access; without it the mic returns silence."
  fix: "Allow it (System Settings > Privacy & Security > Microphone > enable Terminal). speak needs no mic."
linux_portaudio:
  problem: "sounddevice needs system PortAudio on Linux (not bundled in the wheel)."
  fix: "Install libportaudio2 (apt) / portaudio (dnf, pacman)."
piper_platform_wheels:
  note: "piper-tts ships wheels for Windows x64, macOS x64/arm64, Linux x64/aarch64. No Windows ARM64 or Alpine/musl wheel (those build from sdist)."
```
